rationale: The cited paper is topically relevant to the citing paper’s charitable-allocation domain because it examines donors’ tradeoff between attractiveness and neediness (“want” vs. “should”), which could plausibly inform theory about when need drives giving. However, the only linkage evidence provided is a single DOI match (no in-text citation context), and the citing note’s stated contribution centers on goal progress and joint vs. separate evaluation rather than recipient beauty, so the strength of substantive use is unclear.
